response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r4c5 is in critical survival mode: islanded with only 11% SoC (0.33/3 kWh) and 20 minutes of autonomy remaining at current 1.00 kW load. The memory log reveals a microgrid-wide deadlock—all neighbors (r2c3, r3c5) are simultaneously in deficit and requesting energy, while rejecting outbound requests citing their own preservation thresholds. This creates a cascading failure pattern where no peer can afford to share. The repeated cycle of identical requests and rejections over the past 45 minutes indicates the microgrid is trapped in mutual scarcity with no surplus capacity available. Grid reconnection is the only viable path to break this deadlock; holding energy reserves is futile when all peers are equally depleted. Load-shedding must be prioritized immediately to extend autonomy and maximize the window for grid restoration.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Microgrid-wide deadlock detected. All neighbors in deficit; prior sharing attempts rejected. SoC 11% with ~20 min autonomy. Prioritize load-shedding and grid reconnection. Zero outbound sharing until SoC > 50% or grid restored."
ttl_ticks: 5
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
